rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract syntax and structure.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions preserve type consistency and use only visible identifiers.
Write stateful equations with initialized temporal recurrence and branching logic with nested if-then-else.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants exist.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume/guarantee syntax with labeled expressions and eq/const declarations.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Deadline or Period as AGREE variables.
Do not invent new identifiers or types not visible in the current AADL scope.
